title: Equation
description: An Equation is a named, technology-agnostic computation — a pure function defined at the business level. It declares named inputs (referencing Resource Attributes) and a named output. Equations are referenced by Rules and Effects; their concrete implementation is provided by a Script in Technical DNA.

name.description: The snake_case name of the equation, used as a reference key.

description.description: A human-readable explanation of what this equation computes.

input.description: The Attribute names this equation takes as inputs.

output.description: The named result this equation produces.

name.description: The snake_case name of the output value.

type.description: The data type of the output value.

examples.name: calculate_apr
examples.description: Computes the annual percentage rate from loan amount, credit score, and term.

examples.name: score_credit_risk
examples.description: Produces a normalized risk score between 0 and 1 based on applicant attributes.
